name: Current Affairs in Geography of India (9): India's Port Capacity & Maritime Infrastructure (2026)

sourceRefs.label: tarangya.com/visionias.in - India's port capacity (2,762 MTPA, 2026); Mundra Port's 200 MT milestone; Vizhinjam Port's automated terminal; port turnaround-time improvement

researchNotes: Tenth Geography current-affairs topic (9th lap). Deliberately distinct from the Day-70 Economy topic's Coastal Cargo Promotion Scheme mention — this topic focuses on Geography-specific port/coastline facts (capacity, turnaround time, specific port milestones) rather than repeating the already-covered policy scheme.

reading.intro: Covers India's maritime geography fundamentals and 2026 port-infrastructure milestones.

qa.question: What are India's coastline length and Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) area, and what share of GDP does the maritime sector contribute?
qa.answer: Coastline: 11,098 km. EEZ: 2.3 million sq km. Maritime sector contributes about 4% of GDP, with ports handling 95% of India's trade volume.

qa.question: What is India's total port cargo-handling capacity as of 2026, and which port is India's largest commercial port?
qa.answer: 2,762 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) total capacity. Mundra Port is India's largest commercial port — the first Indian port to handle 200 million tonnes (MT) of cargo in a single year.

qa.question: What is distinctive about Vizhinjam Port, Kerala?
qa.answer: It is India's first fully automated deep-water container terminal, designed to handle ultra-large 'megamax' ships, with automated quay cranes and rail-mounted gantries.

qa.question: How has average port turnaround time changed from 2010-11 to FY26?
qa.answer: Fallen from 127 hours (2010-11) to 49.32 hours (FY26) — a major efficiency improvement.

qa.question: What technology milestone did an Indian major port achieve in March 2026?
qa.answer: Became the first port in India to implement Digital Twin technology, enabling real-time monitoring and predictive analysis of port operations.
